title: carthamus glaucus subsp. alexandrinus
intro: Carthamus glaucus subsp. alexandrinus, also known as Alexandrian senna, is a plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The medicinal properties of Alexandrian senna include its ability to act as a purgative due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. The health benefits of using Alexandrian senna include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and lower blood pressure. Alexandrian senna can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or powder, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote bowel health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
